properties.description: Notebook to load data from bronze to Silver Layer and a gold layer with only the latest data

source:
# ============================================================
# TERMINAL CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================

storage_account = "navispowerbidatastorage2"

terminal_folder  = client_id.lower()

# Terminal-specific configurations
TERMINAL_CONFIG = {
    "Malta": {
        "client_key" : 101,
        "datetime_formats": [
           'dd/MM/yyyy H:mm',      # Single-digit hour
    'dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm',     # Double-digit hour  
    'dd/MM/yyyy H:mm:ss',   # Single-digit hour with seconds
    'dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss',  # Double-digit hour with seconds
    'dd/MM/yyyy H:m',       # Single-digit hour and minute
    'dd/MM/yyyy H:m:ss'     # Single-digit hour and minute with seconds
        ],
        "yard_position": {
            "prefix": "Y",
            "pattern": "full",  # full, short, or custom
            "regex_full": r"^Y-[A-Z0-9]+-([A-Z0-9]+)\s([A-Z0-9]+)\s([A-Z0-9]+)([A-Z])$",
            "regex_short": r"^Y-[A-Z0-9]+-([A-Z0-9]+)$",
            "tier_type": "letter"  # letter (A, B, C) or number (1, 2, 3)
        },
        "vessel_position": {
            "prefix": "V",
            "regex": r"^V-[0-9]+-([0-9]+)$",
            "slot_length": 6,  # Expected digits in slot (e.g., 310282)
            "tier_type": "number"
        }
    },
    
    "Terminal2": {
        "datetime_formats": [
            'MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss',  # US format
            'MM/dd/yyyy h:mm:ss a',  # 12-hour with AM/PM
            'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'    # ISO format
        ],
        "yard_position": {
            "prefix": "Y",
            "pattern": "custom",
            "regex_full": r"^Y-([A-Z0-9]+)-([A-Z0-9]+)\s([A-Z0-9]+)$",  # Different format
            "regex_short": r"^Y-([A-Z0-9]+)$",
            "tier_type": "number"  # Uses numbers instead of letters
        },
        "vessel_position": {
            "prefix": "V",
            "regex": r"^V-([0-9]+)-([0-9]+)$",
            "slot_length": 8,  # Different slot length
            "tier_type": "number"
        }
    },
    
    "Terminal3": {
        "datetime_formats": [
            'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss',
            'yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss'
        ],
        "yard_position": {
            "prefix": "Y",
            "pattern": "full",
            "regex_full": r"^Y-[A-Z0-9]+-([A-Z0-9]+)\s([A-Z0-9]+)\s([A-Z0-9]+)([A-Z0-9])$",
            "regex_short": r"^Y-[A-Z0-9]+-([A-Z0-9]+)$",
            "tier_type": "letter"
        },
        "vessel_position": {
            "prefix": "V",
            "regex": r"^V-[0-9]+-([0-9]+)$",
            "slot_length": 6,
            "tier_type": "number"
        }
    }
}
# Get configuration for current terminal
if client_id not in TERMINAL_CONFIG:
    raise ValueError(f"No configuration found for terminal: {client_id}")

config = TERMINAL_CONFIG[client_id]
client_key = config['client_key']



# Paths
bronze_path = f"abfss://{file_system}@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/workinstruction_test/load/"
bronze_archive_path = f"abfss://{file_system}@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/workinstruction_test/archive/"
silver_path = f"abfss://workinstruction@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/silver/{terminal_folder}/"
gold_path = f"abfss://workinstruction@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/gold/WI_AllTerminals/"


print(f"🔄 Processing Terminal: {client_id}")
print(f"📂 Bronze Load Path: {bronze_path}")
print(f"📂 Silver Path: {silver_path}")
print(f"\n⚙️ Terminal Configuration:")
print(f"   • DateTime Formats: {len(config['datetime_formats'])} format(s)")
print(f"   • Yard Prefix: {config['yard_position']['prefix']}")
print(f"   • Yard Tier Type: {config['yard_position']['tier_type']}")
print(f"   • Vessel Prefix: {config['vessel_position']['prefix']}")
print(f"   • Vessel Tier Type: {config['vessel_position']['tier_type']}")
print(f"🔑 Client Key: {client_key}")

# ============================================================
# READ BRONZE CSV FILES
# ============================================================

from pyspark.sql.functions import input_file_name
from notebookutils import mssparkutils

try:
    files = mssparkutils.fs.ls(bronze_path)
    csv_files = [f for f in files if f.name.startswith("workinstruction") and f.name.endswith(".csv")]
    
    if len(csv_files) == 0:
        print("⚠️ No CSV files found in Bronze load folder. Exiting.")
        mssparkutils.notebook.exit('{"status": "NO_FILES", "message": "No CSV files found"}')
    
    print(f"📁 Found {len(csv_files)} CSV file(s):")
    for f in csv_files:
        print(f"   • {f.name}")
        
except Exception as e:
    print(f"❌ Error accessing Bronze folder: {str(e)}")
    raise

bronze_df = spark.read \
    .option("header", "true") \
    .option("inferSchema", "false") \
    .option("delimiter", ",") \
    .option("quote", '"') \
    .option("escape", "\\") \
    .csv(f"{bronze_path}workinstruction*.csv") \
    .withColumn("source_file_name_full", input_file_name())

record_count = bronze_df.count()
print(f"\n✅ Loaded {record_count:,} records from Bronze")

bronze_df.createOrReplaceTempView("bronze_wi")

%%sql
--Column Name Standardization (Same as before)


--(Same as before - no changes needed)
CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP VIEW standardized_wi AS
SELECT 
    `Unit V-State` AS unit_v_state,
    `Assigned Crane` AS assigned_crane,
    `Intended CHE` AS intended_che,
    `State Fetch` AS state_fetch,
    `Unit Type Length` AS unit_type_length,
    `Unit VGM (kg)` AS unit_vgm__kg,
    `Unit uyvGkey` AS unit_uyvgkey,
    `WI Created` AS wi_created_str,
    `WI Modified` AS wi_modified_str,
    `Dest Tz` AS dest_tz,
    `Unit POL` AS unit_pol,
    `Unit Position` AS unit_position,
    `Unit Rfr Type` AS unit_rfr_type,
    `Unit Sparcs Notes` AS unit_sparcs_notes,
    `Unit Stow` AS unit_stow,
    `Unit Stow 2` AS unit_stow_2,
    `Unit Stow 3` AS unit_stow_3,
    `Unit Type ISO` AS unit_type_iso,
    `Unit I/B Actual Visit` AS unit_i_b_actual_visit,
    `Unit Impediments Road` AS unit_impediments_road,
    `Unit Impediments Rail` AS unit_impediments_rail,
    `Unit Impediments Vessel` AS unit_impediments_vessel,
    `Unit ISO Grp` AS unit_iso_grp,
    `Unit Is OOG` AS unit_is_oog,
    `Unit Last Free Day` AS unit_last_free_day,
    `Unit Line Op` AS unit_line_op,
    `Unit Loaded` AS unit_loaded,
    `Unit Must be On-Power` AS unit_must_be_on_power,
    `Unit O/B Actual Visit` AS unit_o_b_actual_visit,
    `Unit On Power` AS unit_on_power,
    `Unit POD` AS unit_pod,
    `Unit Dest` AS unit_dest,
    `Unit Hazardous:0` AS unit_hazardous_0,
    `Unit Damage Severity` AS unit_damage_severity,
    `Unit Category` AS unit_category,
    Locked AS locked,
    `MVHS Time Fetch` AS mvhs_time_fetch_str,
    `State Itv Dispatch` AS state_itv_dispatch,
    Modifier AS modifier,
    `Twist Lock` AS twist_lock,
    `Unit Arrival Booking Number` AS unit_arrival_booking_number,
    `Unit AutoStrad Suitable` AS unit_autostrad_suitable,
    `Unit Arrival Position` AS unit_arrival_position,
    push_rate AS push_rate,
    Queue AS queue,
    mvhs_t_carry_complete AS mvhs_t_carry_complete_str,
    `MVHS Pool Time Put` AS mvhs_pool_time_put_str,
    `EC Hold` AS ec_hold,
    `N4 To Complete` AS n4_to_complete,
    `Nominated CHE` AS nominated_che,
    `Pushrate Offset` AS pushrate_offset,
    `MVHS Time Carry CHE Dispatch` AS mvhs_time_carry_che_dispatch_str,
    `MVHS Rehandle Count` AS mvhs_rehandle_count,
    `Laden Disp. Requested` AS laden_disp__requested,
    Definite AS definite,
    `Confirmed Stage` AS confirmed_stage,
    `Assigned Crane CHE Name` AS assigned_crane_che_name,
    `Unit T-State` AS unit_t_state,
    `Unit Nbr` AS unit_nbr,
    `Yard Move Deadline` AS yard_move_deadline_str,
    Stage AS stage,
    Kind AS kind,
    `WI Position` AS wi_position,
    `Actual Sequence` AS actual_sequence,
    Carrier AS carrier,
    `POW Pow Name` AS pow_pow_name,
    `Queue Seq.` AS queue_seq,
    `MVHS Fetch CHE Id` AS mvhs_fetch_che_id,
    `MVHS Carry CHE Id` AS mvhs_carry_che_id,
    `MVHS Put CHE Id` AS mvhs_put_che_id,
    `Est Mv Time` AS est_mv_time_str,
    status AS status,
    `Twin Fetch` AS twin_fetch,
    `Twin Carry` AS twin_carry,
    `Twin Put` AS twin_put,
    source_file_name_full
FROM bronze_wi

# ============================================================
# DYNAMIC DATE/TIME PARSING
# Build SQL based on terminal configuration
# ============================================================

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, coalesce, to_timestamp

# Get datetime formats for this terminal
datetime_formats = config['datetime_formats']

# Build COALESCE statement for each datetime column
datetime_columns = [
    'wi_created_str',
    'wi_modified_str',
    'yard_move_deadline_str',
    'mvhs_t_carry_complete_str',
    'mvhs_time_fetch_str',
    'mvhs_pool_time_put_str',
    'est_mv_time_str',
    'mvhs_time_carry_che_dispatch_str'
]

# Read standardized data
parsed_df = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM standardized_wi")

# Apply datetime parsing for each column
for dt_col in datetime_columns:
    target_col = dt_col.replace('_str', '')
    
    # Build list of to_timestamp attempts using PySpark functions
    timestamp_attempts = [
        to_timestamp(col(dt_col), fmt) for fmt in datetime_formats
    ]
    
    # Apply COALESCE
    parsed_df = parsed_df.withColumn(target_col, coalesce(*timestamp_attempts))

parsed_df.createOrReplaceTempView("parsed_dates")

print(f"✅ Applied {len(datetime_formats)} datetime format(s) for {len(datetime_columns)} columns")

# ============================================================
# DYNAMIC POSITION PARSING
# Build position parsing based on terminal configuration
# ============================================================

from pyspark.sql.functions import (
    expr, when, col, substring, length, 
    regexp_extract, trim
)

# Get position configuration
yard_config = config['yard_position']
vessel_config = config['vessel_position']

# Read parsed dates
position_df = spark.sql("SELECT * FROM parsed_dates")

# ============================================================
# Pre-build regex patterns for position_3 (avoid backslash in f-string)
# ============================================================

yard_regex_full = yard_config['regex_full']
yard_regex_short = yard_config['regex_short']
vessel_regex = vessel_config['regex']

# Build regex for 2-part and 3-part extraction
yard_regex_2parts = yard_regex_full.replace('([A-Z0-9]+)\\s([A-Z0-9]+)\\s([A-Z0-9]+)', r'([A-Z0-9]+\s[A-Z0-9]+)')
yard_regex_3parts = yard_regex_full.replace('([A-Z0-9]+)', r'([A-Z0-9]+\s[A-Z0-9]+\s[A-Z0-9]+)')

# ============================================================
# UNIT POSITION PARSING
# ============================================================

# Position Type
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "unit_position_type",
    substring(trim(col("unit_position")), 1, 1)  # First character
)

# Position 1: Block (Yard) or Bay (Vessel)
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "unit_position_1",
    when(
        (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") > 0),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("unit_position")), yard_regex_full, 1)
    ).when(
        (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") == 0),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("unit_position")), yard_regex_short, 1)
    ).when(
        trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        substring(regexp_extract(trim(col("unit_position")), vessel_regex, 1), 1, 2)
    ).otherwise('')
)

# Position 2: Block+Stack (Yard) or Full Slot (Vessel)
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "unit_position_2",
    when(
        (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") > 0),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("unit_position")), yard_regex_2parts, 1)
    ).when(
        trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("unit_position")), vessel_regex, 1)
    ).otherwise('')
)

# Position 3: Slot without tier
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "unit_position_3",
    when(
        (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") > 0),
        expr(f"substring(regexp_extract(trim(unit_position), '{yard_regex_3parts}', 1), 1, length(regexp_extract(trim(unit_position), '{yard_regex_3parts}', 1)) - 1)")
    ).when(
        trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        substring(regexp_extract(trim(col("unit_position")), vessel_regex, 1), 1, 4)
    ).otherwise('')
)

# Position 4: Tier (last character)
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "unit_position_4",
    when(
        (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") > 0),
        expr("substring(trim(unit_position), -1, 1)")  # Last character
    ).when(
        trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        expr(f"substring(regexp_extract(trim(unit_position), '{vessel_regex}', 1), -2, 2)")  # Last 2 chars
    ).otherwise('')
)

# Tier Number
if yard_config['tier_type'] == 'letter':
    # Letter tiers: A=1, B=2, C=3
    position_df = position_df.withColumn(
        "unit_tier_num",
        when(
            (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
            (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") > 0),
            expr("ascii(substring(trim(unit_position), -1, 1)) - ascii('A') + 1")
        ).when(
            trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
            expr(f"cast(substring(regexp_extract(trim(unit_position), '{vessel_regex}', 1), -2, 2) as int)")
        ).otherwise(0)
    )
else:
    # Number tiers
    position_df = position_df.withColumn(
        "unit_tier_num",
        when(
            (trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
            (expr("locate(' ', trim(unit_position))") > 0),
            expr("cast(substring(trim(unit_position), -1, 1) as int)")
        ).when(
            trim(col("unit_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
            expr(f"cast(substring(regexp_extract(trim(unit_position), '{vessel_regex}', 1), -2, 2) as int)")
        ).otherwise(0)
    )

# ============================================================
# WI POSITION PARSING (Same logic)
# ============================================================

position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "wi_position_type", 
    substring(trim(col("wi_position")), 1, 1)
)

# wi_position_1
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "wi_position_1",
    when(
        (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") > 0),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("wi_position")), yard_regex_full, 1)
    ).when(
        (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") == 0),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("wi_position")), yard_regex_short, 1)
    ).when(
        trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        substring(regexp_extract(trim(col("wi_position")), vessel_regex, 1), 1, 2)
    ).otherwise('')
)

# wi_position_2
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "wi_position_2",
    when(
        (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") > 0),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("wi_position")), yard_regex_2parts, 1)
    ).when(
        trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        regexp_extract(trim(col("wi_position")), vessel_regex, 1)
    ).otherwise('')
)

# wi_position_3
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "wi_position_3",
    when(
        (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") > 0),
        expr(f"substring(regexp_extract(trim(wi_position), '{yard_regex_3parts}', 1), 1, length(regexp_extract(trim(wi_position), '{yard_regex_3parts}', 1)) - 1)")
    ).when(
        trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        substring(regexp_extract(trim(col("wi_position")), vessel_regex, 1), 1, 4)
    ).otherwise('')
)

# wi_position_4
position_df = position_df.withColumn(
    "wi_position_4",
    when(
        (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
        (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") > 0),
        expr("substring(trim(wi_position), -1, 1)")
    ).when(
        trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
        expr(f"substring(regexp_extract(trim(wi_position), '{vessel_regex}', 1), -2, 2)")
    ).otherwise('')
)

# wi_tier_num
if yard_config['tier_type'] == 'letter':
    position_df = position_df.withColumn(
        "wi_tier_num",
        when(
            (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
            (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") > 0),
            expr("ascii(substring(trim(wi_position), -1, 1)) - ascii('A') + 1")
        ).when(
            trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
            expr(f"cast(substring(regexp_extract(trim(wi_position), '{vessel_regex}', 1), -2, 2) as int)")
        ).otherwise(0)
    )
else:
    position_df = position_df.withColumn(
        "wi_tier_num",
        when(
            (trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(yard_config['prefix'])) & 
            (expr("locate(' ', trim(wi_position))") > 0),
            expr("cast(substring(trim(wi_position), -1, 1) as int)")
        ).when(
            trim(col("wi_position")).startswith(vessel_config['prefix']),
            expr(f"cast(substring(regexp_extract(trim(wi_position), '{vessel_regex}', 1), -2, 2) as int)")
        ).otherwise(0)
    )

position_df.createOrReplaceTempView("with_positions")

print(f"✅ Applied position parsing for terminal: {client_id}")
print(f"   • Yard pattern: {yard_config['pattern']}")
print(f"   • Yard tier type: {yard_config['tier_type']}")
print(f"   • Vessel tier type: {vessel_config['tier_type']}")

# ============================================================
# ADD REMAINING DERIVED COLUMNS
# ============================================================

spark.sql(f"""
CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP VIEW with_derived AS

SELECT 
    *,
    
    -- Integration key
    CONCAT(unit_nbr, '_', DATE_FORMAT(wi_modified, 'ddMMyyyyHHmmss')) AS integration_key,
    
    -- Client identifiers
    '{client_id}' AS client_id,
    {client_key} AS client_key,
    
    -- Load timestamp
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() AS load_timestamp,
    
    -- Source file name extraction
    REGEXP_EXTRACT(source_file_name_full, '(\\\\d{{8}}_\\\\d{{4}})', 1) AS source_file_name,
    
    -- Source file sort key
    CAST(CONCAT(
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(source_file_name_full, '(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{4}})_(\\\\d{{4}})', 3),
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(source_file_name_full, '(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{4}})_(\\\\d{{4}})', 2),
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(source_file_name_full, '(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{4}})_(\\\\d{{4}})', 1),
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(source_file_name_full, '(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{2}})(\\\\d{{4}})_(\\\\d{{4}})', 4)
    ) AS BIGINT) AS source_file_name_sort,
    
    -- Date parts
    YEAR(wi_modified) AS year,
    MONTH(wi_modified) AS month,
    WEEKOFYEAR(wi_modified) AS week
    
FROM with_positions
""")

print(f"✅ Derived columns added")
print(f"   • client_id: {client_id}")
print(f"   • client_key: {client_key}")

%%sql
-- ============================================================
-- DEDUPLICATION
-- Keep latest record per integration_key based on source_file_name_sort
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP VIEW deduplicated AS

SELECT 
    *,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY integration_key 
        ORDER BY source_file_name_sort DESC
    ) AS row_number
FROM with_derived
WHERE integration_key IS NOT NULL

# ============================================================
# FINAL SILVER DATA (Filter row_number = 1)
# ============================================================

silver_df = spark.sql("""
    SELECT 
        -- All original columns
        unit_v_state,
        assigned_crane,
        intended_che,
        state_fetch,
        unit_type_length,
        unit_vgm__kg,
        unit_uyvgkey,
        wi_created,
        wi_modified,
        dest_tz,
        unit_pol,
        unit_position,
        unit_rfr_type,
        unit_sparcs_notes,
        unit_stow,
        unit_stow_2,
        unit_stow_3,
        unit_type_iso,
        unit_i_b_actual_visit,
        unit_impediments_road,
        unit_impediments_rail,
        unit_impediments_vessel,
        unit_iso_grp,
        unit_is_oog,
        unit_last_free_day,
        unit_line_op,
        unit_loaded,
        unit_must_be_on_power,
        unit_o_b_actual_visit,
        unit_on_power,
        unit_pod,
        unit_dest,
        unit_hazardous_0,
        unit_damage_severity,
        unit_category,
        locked,
        mvhs_time_fetch,
        state_itv_dispatch,
        modifier,
        twist_lock,
        unit_arrival_booking_number,
        unit_autostrad_suitable,
        unit_arrival_position,
        push_rate,
        queue,
        mvhs_t_carry_complete,
        mvhs_pool_time_put,
        ec_hold,
        n4_to_complete,
        nominated_che,
        pushrate_offset,
        mvhs_time_carry_che_dispatch,
        mvhs_rehandle_count,
        laden_disp__requested,
        definite,
        confirmed_stage,
        assigned_crane_che_name,
        unit_t_state,
        unit_nbr,
        yard_move_deadline,
        stage,
        kind,
        wi_position,
        actual_sequence,
        carrier,
        pow_pow_name,
        queue_seq,
        mvhs_fetch_che_id,
        mvhs_carry_che_id,
        mvhs_put_che_id,
        est_mv_time,
        status,
        twin_fetch,
        twin_carry,
        twin_put,
        
        -- Derived columns
        source_file_name_full AS sourcefilename,
        integration_key,
        client_id,
        client_key,
        load_timestamp,
        source_file_name,
        source_file_name_sort,
        unit_position_type,
        unit_position_1,
        unit_position_2,
        unit_position_3,
        unit_position_4,
        unit_tier_num,
        wi_position_type,
        wi_position_1,
        wi_position_2,
        wi_position_3,
        wi_position_4,
        wi_tier_num,
        year,
        month,
        week
        
    FROM deduplicated
    WHERE row_number = 1
""")

final_count = silver_df.count()
print(f"✅ Final Silver records (after deduplication): {final_count:,}")

# ============================================================
# WRITE TO SILVER LAYER
# ============================================================

from delta.tables import DeltaTable

try:
    if DeltaTable.isDeltaTable(spark, silver_path):
        # Append new data
        silver_df.write \
            .format("delta") \
            .mode("append") \
            .option("mergeSchema", "true") \
            .save(silver_path)
        
        print(f"✅ Silver: Appended {final_count:,} records for '{client_id}'")
    else:
        # First run - create table
        silver_df.write \
            .format("delta") \
            .mode("overwrite") \
            .option("mergeSchema", "true") \
            .save(silver_path)
        
        print(f"✅ Silver: Created table with {final_count:,} records for '{client_id}'")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"❌ Failed to write to Silver: {str(e)}")
    raise

# ============================================================
# WRITE TO GOLD LAYER (All Terminals - Latest Data)
# ============================================================

import pyspark.sql.functions as F
from delta.tables import DeltaTable

# Get latest file for this terminal
latest_sort = silver_df \
    .agg(F.max("source_file_name_sort")) \
    .collect()[0][0]

# Filter to latest data only
df_gold = silver_df.filter(F.col("source_file_name_sort") == latest_sort)

gold_record_count = df_gold.count()

print(f"📊 Preparing Gold write for terminal: {client_id}")
print(f"   • Latest file sort: {latest_sort}")
print(f"   • Records to write: {gold_record_count:,}")

try:
    if DeltaTable.isDeltaTable(spark, gold_path):
        # Table exists → Use dynamic partition overwrite
        print(f"✅ Gold table exists - using dynamic partition overwrite")
        
        # Dynamic partition overwrite — only touches this terminal's partition
        spark.conf.set("spark.sql.sources.partitionOverwriteMode", "dynamic")
        
        df_gold.write \
            .format("delta") \
            .mode("overwrite") \
            .partitionBy("client_id") \
            .option("mergeSchema", "true") \
            .save(gold_path)
        
        print(f"✅ Gold partition replaced for client_id = '{client_id}'")
        
    else:
        # First run → Create new table with partitioning
        print(f"📦 Gold table does not exist - creating new table")
        
        df_gold.write \
            .format("delta") \
            .mode("overwrite") \
            .partitionBy("client_id") \
            .option("mergeSchema", "true") \
            .save(gold_path)
        
        print(f"✅ Gold table created with data for '{client_id}'")
    
    print(f"📁 Partition: client_id={client_id}")
    print(f"🔢 File sort: {latest_sort}")
    print(f"📊 Records written: {gold_record_count:,}")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"❌ Failed to write to Gold: {str(e)}")
    raise

# ============================================================
# ARCHIVE BRONZE FILES
# ============================================================

from notebookutils import mssparkutils

try:
    files = mssparkutils.fs.ls(bronze_path)
    csv_files = [f for f in files if f.name.startswith("workinstruction") and f.name.endswith(".csv")]
    
    archived_count = 0
    for file in csv_files:
        source = file.path
        destination = f"{bronze_archive_path}{file.name}"
        mssparkutils.fs.mv(source, destination, True)
        print(f"📦 Archived: {file.name}")
        archived_count += 1
    
    print(f"\n✅ Archived {archived_count} file(s)")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"⚠️ Warning: Failed to archive files: {str(e)}")
    # Don't fail the pipeline if archiving fails
